title: Cutting cordwood; swapping yarns; how to saw a crosscut
description: Cutting cordwood. Ten and a half cords in three days with a crosscut saw. Swapping yarns. Ulysses shows a wise-off kid that there's more to sawing than there seems. How to saw with a crosscut. Showing Stanley that there's more to sawing than filing the saw.

title: L.K. Stron hired 500 cords cut every winter; buying a shack; playing mumbletyped; logging camps
description: L.K. Strong had lots of timber and would hire 500 cords cut every winter. Ulysses buys a shack for $15. $1 to $1.25 per cord. Playing mumbletyped. Logging camps.

title: Moving West; looking for the Promised Land; father getting knifed
description: Moving West, looking for the Promised Land. Ulysses' father gets knifed by a mean Tennessean after an argument,

title: Thrashing season; steam-whistles; sack-sewing
description: During thrashing season you could hear steam-whistles blowing all over the countryside. Sack-sewing.

title: Sack-sewing; wrestling, tussling, and tricking
description: Sack-sewing (continued). Wrestling, tussling, and tricking. Giving a bully his comeuppance.

title: Starting moonshining; moonshine cured ulcers; hiding moonshine in town; turned in by a stool pigeon
description: Ulysses started moonshining because he liked to drink. Buying a still. Moonshine cured Ulysses of ulcers; fun and profit. Competition. Running moonshine to town and hiding it there. Pinters. Turned in by a stool pigeon. Busted.

title: Women drinking and smoking during the Prohibition; got out of prison; John Quist paying drunk fines
description: Women took up drinking and smoking during Prohibition. Saloon trade moved into houses. Drinking increased during Prohibition. After Ulysses got out of prison, he found out who really had Christian forgiveness. John Quist says he paved the town paying drunk fines.

title: Team poker; poker ethics; marking cards
description: Playing poker with marked cards. Team poker. Poker ethics.
